title: My times
quote: You are not behind. The hand holding your times has a different watch.
body:
My times are in your hand. The verse is short. It is also enough.

End of May. I used to be embarrassed at the end of every month. I had not done what I had planned. The list was longer at the end than at the beginning. The accounting was always against me.

Then I read this verse and stopped doing the accounting. My times are not on a project schedule. They are in a hand. A hand that has not been surprised by what May actually held. A hand that is not late.

Today: hand the month back. Let the next one begin in the right hand.

title: There remains
quote: Rest is not what is left over. It is what is being kept for you.
body:
So then, there remains a Sabbath rest for the people of God. The verse uses a word of preservation. The rest is not used up. It is kept.

I have lived as if rest had a queue. As if I was waiting my turn behind everyone more deserving. The verse is offering something else. There is a kept rest. With your name on it. It does not run out. It does not get given to someone faster.

You do not have to earn the seat. You have to claim it. The kept thing is only useful if the person it was kept for actually shows up.

Today: enter the rest that has been kept for you. Not earned. Kept.

title: Endurance
quote: The suffering is not the point. What it is making in you is the point.
body:
We rejoice in our sufferings, knowing that suffering produces endurance. Paul does not call suffering good. He calls it productive. Different word. Different claim.

The hardest seasons of my life have made me. Not because they were beautiful. They were not. Because I did not get to skip them, and the skipping was what would have kept me small. The endurance Paul is talking about is built only one way. By going through.

There is no shortcut to a built thing. The bypass road takes you to a different country than the one you were heading to.

Today: do not look for the exit ramp. Walk through.

title: The truth
quote: A managed truth becomes a small lie that grows interest.
body:
Therefore, having put away falsehood, let each one of you speak the truth with his neighbor. The verse is not abstract. It is operational. Speak the truth. Stop managing the version. Stop curating the perception.

I spent years in conversations where the truth got managed before it left my mouth. The managed version did not save anyone. It just delayed the conversation we were eventually going to have. By the time we had it, it cost more than it would have cost on day one.

The interest on a withheld truth compounds. Pay the principal early.

Today: say the unmanaged sentence. The cheaper version is now.

title: Day by day
quote: Something inside you is being built faster than something outside you is being lost.
body:
So we do not lose heart. Though our outer self is wasting away, our inner self is being renewed day by day. The verse names two things at the same time. Wasting. Renewing. Both true. Both happening in the same body.

I notice the wasting. The hairline. The slower morning. The old injury that wakes up before I do. I do not always notice the renewing. It is quieter. It happens at a depth I cannot photograph. But it happens. Day by day. Below the visible.

The one being renewed will outlast the one being lost. That is the math the verse is doing.

Today: trust the renewal you cannot see. It is doing more than the wasting you can.

title: Awake without afraid
quote: Awake is not the same as anxious. One is alert. The other is exhausted.
body:
Let us not sleep, as others do, but let us keep awake and be sober. Paul calls for both. Awake. Sober. Steady.

There is a difference between being awake and being on edge. Awake is alert without being afraid. Sober is steady without being numb. Most people are one or the other. The work is to be both.

I have spent seasons asleep when I should have been awake. I have spent other seasons on alarm when I should have been at peace. The right posture is rare and worth practicing.

Today: be awake without being afraid. Watch what matters. Stop watching what does not.

title: Press on
quote: The goal is not behind you. Stop running backwards to argue with what you already left.
body:
I press on toward the goal for the prize of the upward call of God in Christ Jesus. The verse is forward.

I lose months, sometimes years, re-litigating what is behind me. The bad season. The lost relationship. The version of me that did not know yet. Paul will not let me stay there. Press on. The prize is ahead, not on the autopsy table behind you.

Forward is not denial. It is an act of trust. The behind is behind on purpose. The Lord put it there.

Today: face forward. Run toward, not from.

title: The anchor
quote: An anchor does not stop the storm. It just keeps you from drifting through it.
body:
We have this as a sure and steadfast anchor of the soul. The verse does not promise still water. It promises an anchor. The water can do whatever it is going to do. The anchor will hold.

I have been in storms where the only honest thing I could say was that I had not drifted. I had not made peace with the storm. I had not even understood it. I had just held. That holding was not me. It was the anchor.

The weight you cannot lift is being held by something heavier than you. That is the design.

Today: trust the anchor. You do not have to also be the anchor.

title: More than all
quote: God is not measuring the size of the gift. He is measuring what was left in your hand after.
body:
Truly, I say to you, this poor widow has put in more than all those who are contributing to the offering box. The math is not the math we do. The widow gave less in absolute terms and more in everything-she-had terms. Jesus saw the second number, not the first.

I have given a lot of things from my surplus and called it generous. Surplus generosity is not nothing. It is also not what Jesus is pointing at. He is pointing at the cost.

The gift that costs you nothing is closer to a transaction than to worship.

Today: notice what something would actually cost you. Then consider whether you are willing.

title: The narrow gate
quote: The narrow road is not narrow because God is stingy. It is narrow because most people are not actually willing.
body:
For the gate is narrow and the way is hard that leads to life, and those who find it are few. Hard. Few. Jesus is not selling. He is reporting.

I used to flinch at this verse. Then I started to find it kind. He could have lied. He could have advertised an easy gate and a wide road. He told the truth. The truth was: this is going to cost you, and most people will not be willing to pay.

The reward is on the other side of a road most people declined to take. That is not stinginess. That is honest accounting.

Today: count the cost honestly. Pay it anyway. The road is real.

title: Salt
quote: The point of salt is not to be noticed. It is to make the meal taste like itself.
body:
You are the salt of the earth. But if salt has lost its taste, how shall its saltiness be restored? Salt does not draw attention to itself. It makes the food taste more like food. That is the work.

I have wanted my faith to look impressive. Jesus is offering a quieter assignment. Be the thing in the room that makes the room taste truer. You may never be the headline. You will be the reason someone leaves the meal nourished.

The salt you cannot taste is the salt doing its job.

Today: be the salt. Quietly. Without explaining yourself.

title: Light
quote: Light is not loud. It just refuses to be hidden.
body:
You are the light of the world. A city set on a hill cannot be hidden. Jesus does not say try to be the light. He says you are. The work is not generation. The work is not concealment.

I have spent seasons hiding. Dimming. Apologizing for what I was. The dimming did not protect anyone. It just made the room darker than it needed to be.

There are people in your life waiting on a light you have been talking yourself out of being.

Today: stop dimming. The room is asking for more light, not less.

title: The vine
quote: Connection is not optional equipment. It is the equipment.
body:
I am the vine, you are the branches. Whoever abides in me and I in him, he it is that bears much fruit, for apart from me you can do nothing. Apart from me you can do nothing. Jesus does not soften it.

I have proved this verse more times than I would like. The seasons I tried to bear fruit by my own discipline produced the kind of fruit that did not last. The seasons I stayed connected, even when nothing felt like it was happening, produced a different kind. The slow kind. The kind that survived.

The branch is not impressive. It is also not the point. The branch is just connected.

Today: stay connected. That is the whole job description.

title: Very early
quote: The resurrection happened before anyone arrived to record it. That is the pattern.
body:
Very early on the first day of the week, when the sun had risen, they went to the tomb. The women came expecting a body. They did not come expecting good news. They came expecting grief work. The grief work is what was already finished by the time they got there.

I have shown up at a lot of tombs in my life with the wrong assumption. That what I was about to find would be what I had left there. Sometimes God had moved before I came to verify the loss.

The stone you are dreading may already be rolled away. Walk closer.

Today: walk toward what you were dreading. It may already be different than you remember.

title: The release
quote: You are not the warden. You can leave the cell whenever you want.
body:
Be kind to one another, tenderhearted, forgiving one another, as God in Christ forgave you. Forgiveness is not a feeling. It is a release. You stop carrying the file. You stop pulling it out at family dinners. You stop rehearsing the verdict you wanted to deliver.

The other person may not know you forgave them. That is fine. The release was not for them. It was for you. The cell door was unlocked the whole time.

The weight you have been carrying as proof of how much it hurt is not actually proving anything. It is just heavy.

Today: open one cell. Walk out. Do not look back to make sure the bars are still there.

title: The asking
quote: You will not always get the answer you wanted. You will always get the conversation.
body:
Ask, and it will be given to you. Seek, and you will find. Knock, and it will be opened. The verbs are continuous. Keep asking. Keep seeking. Keep knocking. The verse is not promising a vending machine. It is promising a relationship.

I used to give up on asking when the first answer was no. Now I see no as a chapter, not the book. The asking is the relationship, even when the asking does not get me what I asked for.

The Father is not annoyed by your repetition. He is making something in you through it.

Today: ask again. Do not wait for proof of arrival before you make the next request.

title: The whole story
quote: Being known is not exposed. It is held.
body:
O Lord, you have searched me and known me. Searched. The word is intentional. Not glanced at. Not skimmed. Searched. The deep look of someone trying to actually understand the room.

I have been afraid of the searched look most of my life. I assumed it would publish a verdict. The verdict came and it was not what I feared. It was: known. Held. Still loved.

The One looking is not gathering evidence. He is gathering you in.

Today: let yourself be searched and discover you are still kept.

title: The shepherd
quote: A shepherd is the one who counts you as a single number, not a percentage.
body:
I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep. A shepherd does not lose a number. He loses a face. The single missing one is not a rounding error.

I have been a number on a lot of dashboards. The shepherd's math is different. He does not aggregate me. He sees me. By name. With my specific limp. With the particular ditch I always wander into.

You are not in a herd. You are in His gaze.

Today: be seen by the One who is not aggregating you.

title: Run to win
quote: There is a difference between participating and competing for the prize.
body:
So run that you may obtain it. Paul does not soften the verb.

I have a long history of running races I had already decided I would not win. Showing up at half-attention. Treating the day as a holding pattern instead of a competition for something. Survival as the bar.

Paul will not let it stay there. Run. To obtain. Today. The prize is not an abstraction. It is real, and it is worth your full chest.

Today: do at least one thing as if the prize were real.

title: Daily bread
quote: Tomorrow is not on the menu. Today is.
body:
Give us this day our daily bread. The prayer is small. That is the design.

Not give us this week our weekly bread. Not give us this month our monthly bread. Daily. One day. One supply. One enough.

I keep trying to ration what I have not received yet. To pre-worry the bread of next Wednesday. The prayer Jesus taught is not anxious. It is precise. Today's bread, today.

Today: ask for one day's worth. Receive it. Eat it. Trust that the next day will have its own bread.

title: Her name
quote: Her work was the kind nobody applauded. Then everyone realized none of it would have held without her.
body:
Her children rise up and call her blessed. Her husband also, and he praises her. The verse does not say her résumé. It says her children. It says her husband. The credit is given by the people who actually lived with her.

My mother. My wife. The mothers in my life were the load-bearing walls that did not get featured on the architectural tour. The house held because of them, even when the house did not say so.

The quiet faithfulness of a woman who keeps showing up is one of the most underrated structures in the world. It deserves better than silence.

Today: say it. Out loud. To the woman who has been holding the structure together while no one was naming her.

title: At all times
quote: A friend is not a person who liked the easy version of you. It is the one who stayed for the rest.
body:
A friend loves at all times, and a brother is born for adversity. The verse names the test. At all times. Not in the chapters where you were impressive. Not when you were the easy version of yourself.

I have a small number of these people. They do not always say much. They do not need to. Their faithfulness is the sentence. Their refusal to leave when the room got hard is the love.

This is also a job description, not just a category. You are someone's friend. At all times.

Today: be the friend at all times. To one person. Today. Not metaphorically.

title: Slow to speak
quote: You will not be remembered for what you said in the heated minute. You will be remembered for the silence you held instead.
body:
Be quick to hear, slow to speak, slow to anger. The verse is in that order on purpose.

I have lost more arguments by speaking first than by speaking second. The silence that lasts a beat longer than is comfortable is one of the most powerful things in a marriage, a conversation, a meeting, a prayer.

Most of the regrets I carry are from sentences I said before I had finished hearing.

Today: leave the gap. Do not fill it with a word you will spend the rest of the week un-saying.

title: Sufficient
quote: Grace is not the size of what is given. It is the precision of how it fits.
body:
My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness. Sufficient is not exciting. It is not abundant. It is not lavish. It is enough. Just enough. Today's enough.

I wanted overflow. I wanted to feel cushioned. The cushion did not come. What came was sufficient. Grace for the hour. Grace for the email. Grace for the difficult sentence I had to say without flinching. Not a stockpile. A supply.

The weakness is not the obstacle. It is the place the power gets to be visible.

Today: stop asking for the stockpile. Receive the supply.

title: Let it finish
quote: Most things in your life broke because you stopped them just before they could form.
body:
Let steadfastness have its full effect, that you may be perfect and complete, lacking in nothing. The verse is about not interrupting. Not pulling the bread out of the oven at minute fourteen. Not ending the difficult conversation before the thing under it has surfaced.

I have ruined a lot of bread. I have rushed a lot of conversations. I have folded perfectly good seasons because I could not stand the middle.

The full effect requires you to not yank the thing out at the discomfort point. The discomfort is the part where the forming happens.

Today: do not stop the thing two minutes early. Let it finish.

title: The birds
quote: You will not pray your way out of worry. You will only practice your way out of it.
body:
Look at the birds of the air. They neither sow nor reap nor gather into barns, and yet your heavenly Father feeds them.

Jesus does not argue with worry. He just points at something. Look at the birds. The argument is in what you see, not what you say.

I have not yet won an argument with my own anxiety. I have, on better days, looked at something else long enough that the anxiety lost its volume. The bird outside the window. The kid in the next room. The way the light came in.

Today: look at something that is not the worry. Long enough to forget what you were holding.

title: Sixty days
quote: The race is not won by speed. It is won by who is still running on day sixty.
body:
Let us run with endurance the race that is set before us. The verse does not promise a sprint. It promises a course.

Sixty days of these readings. I did not know I would still be writing them at sixty. The honest truth is I did not know I would still be doing a lot of things at sixty days. Most of what I started in my life did not survive the second month.

The ones that did were not the most exciting. They were the ones I let be ordinary. The ones I did not need to feel inspired to do. The ones that became a small architecture under the day.

Today: keep going at the thing that has stopped feeling like anything. That is the part where it counts.

title: Today, again
quote: The day is not asking you to make it remarkable. It is asking you to make it yours.
body:
This is the day the Lord has made. We will rejoice and be glad in it. The verse is short on conditions.

Not the day after the breakthrough. Not the day after you sleep well. Not the day after the apology lands. This day. The one with the half-finished coffee and the email you have been avoiding.

I have read this verse on bad days and felt it as a demand. Then I started reading it as a gift. The day was made. By Someone. For you. The rejoicing is not the decoration. It is the point.

Today: receive it before you fix it.

title: The slow Saturday
quote: Rest is not the reward for finishing. It is the rhythm that makes finishing possible.
body:
The Sabbath was made for man, not man for the Sabbath. Jesus says it twice in different ways so we will not miss it.

I spent years treating rest as a reward I had not earned yet. Finish the list, then sit down. Catch up on email, then breathe. The list never finished. The breath never came.

Saturday is not a delay. It is a design. The body needs it. The mind needs it. The relationship to God needs it. Not because you are weak. Because you are made.

Today: rest before you have earned it. That is the entire point.

title: The new thing
quote: The new thing is rarely a new room. It is usually the same room with new eyes.
body:
Behold, I am doing a new thing. The verse is in present tense. Not will do. Not someday. Now.

I used to want the new thing to look new. A different city. A different career. A different version of the day. Most of the new things God has done in me did not change my address. They changed how I walked into the same kitchen.

May begins today. A new month is not a new life. It is a new look at the one you already have.

Today: notice what is being made new in the room you are already in.

title: At the end of the month
quote: To number our days is not morbid. It is how we learn to spend them.
body:
Teach us to number our days, that we may get a heart of wisdom. A month is ending. A small threshold. Easy to miss.

I used to wait for the big markers. A new year, a decade, a funeral. As if wisdom only arrived with weight. The verse says something quieter. Count the days. Just the days.

Thirty of them just went by. Some were ordinary. Some were hard. Some you will not remember by Friday. But they were given. That is reason enough to stop and say so.

Today: close the month honestly. Count what was given. Begin tomorrow clean.

title: Not your strength
quote: The strength that actually holds you is not the one you manufactured.
body:
Not by might, not by power, but by my Spirit, says the Lord. The verse is a relief if you let it be one.

I spent a long season trying to rebuild by will. More discipline. More grit. More hours before anyone else was up. Some of that was good. Most of it was me trying to prove to God that I could earn the rebuilding He was already doing for free.

The rebuild that holds is not the one I powered. It is the one I received. Every time I have tried to carry it alone, it has gotten heavier. Every time I have let it be His, it has gotten lighter without being lighter.

Today: notice where you are still trying to lift what was never yours to lift.

title: The long obedience
quote: Your faithfulness is not wasted. It is doing work you cannot see.
body:
As the rain comes down and waters the earth. The verse does not promise you will see the return. It promises the return is happening.

I have planted a lot of things that did not look like they took. The emails I sent that went nowhere. The conversations I had that seemed to change nothing. The prayers that felt like talking to a ceiling. I wanted visible growth, and sometimes there was none. That did not mean nothing was happening.

Rain does not announce itself. It just works. By the time you notice the green, the rain has already finished its job and moved on.

Today: do the unseen work. Trust the slow return.

title: The table
quote: You did not earn the table. You were invited to it.
body:
You prepare a table before me. The verse does not say I set the table. Someone else set it. I just sat down.

I have a long history of trying to earn my seat at a table that was already set for me. I brought dishes nobody asked me to bring. I rearranged chairs. I apologized for being there. None of it was the posture the table was designed for.

The posture is received. Sit. Eat. Be fed. The table is a gift, not a performance review.

Today: stop rearranging what was prepared. Sit down.

title: The still voice
quote: God was not in the earthquake. God was in the small quiet that came after.
body:
Not in the wind. Not in the earthquake. Not in the fire. A still small voice.

I used to want God to show up loud. A sign big enough that I could not miss it. Certainty I could point at. Then the dramatic moment passed and I realized I had been standing in the still voice the whole time, waiting for a different one.

God speaks in the quiet most often. The unrushed morning. The sentence that arrives before you have finished your coffee. The thought you almost dismissed because it did not sound important enough.

Today: be quiet long enough to hear what has been speaking the whole time.

title: The yoke
quote: A yoke is not the absence of weight. It is a way to carry it without breaking.
body:
Come to me all you who are weary. The verse does not promise the weight goes away. It promises a different way of carrying.

I spent years believing rest meant no responsibility. Then I read the verse again and saw what Jesus actually offered. Not the removal of the yoke. A better yoke. One shaped for two.

You are carrying something. Maybe it is the thing that has been on you for a long time. Maybe it is a newer thing. Either way, you were not built to carry it alone with a yoke shaped only for you.

Today: find the second shoulder. Let something be shared.

title: The hidden work
quote: The work that counts is often the work nobody is watching.
body:
Your life is hidden with Christ in God. That is the verse. I used to read it and feel vaguely uncomfortable, like I was being asked to accept obscurity.

Then I started to see it the other way. Hidden is not a demotion. Hidden is a location. It is where the real work gets done, before anyone hands you a microphone or tells you it counts. The years you think are invisible are the years something is being formed that will hold up later when visibility comes.

Most of what made me was not photographed. It did not trend. It happened in rooms nobody saw. If God has you in one of those rooms, do not assume you are being forgotten. You are being built.

Today: do the hidden thing. Let it count without a witness.

title: The small yes
quote: You do not have a big-faith problem. You have a small-yes problem.
body:
Whoever is faithful in very little is also faithful in much.

I wanted the big yes. The launch, the call, the moment I would know for sure. I kept waiting for a door to open wide enough that I would not have to guess whether to walk through it. That door did not come. What came was the small yes, over and over, and the quiet question of whether I was going to take it seriously.

The small yes is the text you finally send. The apology you stop rehearsing and actually say. The fifteen minutes you give to the prayer you keep postponing. The one honest sentence to your wife instead of the managed version. These do not feel like anything. They feel like nothing. They are not nothing.

Faithfulness is not built in the moments you expected it to be tested. It is built in the ones you almost did not notice were a test at all.

Today: find the small yes. Say it. Count it.

title: The ordinary hours
quote: The holy is not elsewhere. It is the hour you were going to call boring.
body:
Whatever you do, do it with all your heart, as working for the Lord, not for men.

I used to read that verse as a motivation line. Work harder. Show up better. I think I was reading the wrong word. The word is whatever. The verse does not promise you an impressive assignment. It promises that the unimpressive one counts.

Most of your life will be ordinary hours. The meeting that was not remarkable. The dinner that was the same dinner. The prayer that felt like nothing. These are not the waiting room for the real life. They are the life.

Today: do the next thing with your whole chest. Not because it matters to anyone watching. Because Someone is.

title: The quiet rebuild
quote: Grace does not wait for your readiness. It arrives before you have cleaned up.
body:
This is the quiet theology of the rebuild. You do not earn your way back into the room. You are carried in, and then you are asked to stay.

Today, do the small, unglamorous thing. Count it. Go again tomorrow. The foundation is being laid whether you can see it or not. That is not a metaphor. That is the mechanism.

title: Grace before readiness
quote: God did not wait until you had your life together. He moved while you were still making the mess.
body:
The verse does not say while we were trying. It says while we were still sinners. That is not a metaphor. That is a timing detail.

Grace is not a reward for reaching a certain temperature of readiness. It is what arrives in the cold room before the heat comes back on. I spent years trying to clean up before I came. The cleanup was not the requirement. It was the avoidance.

Today: let something be enough before it is finished.

title: What the body carried
quote: The body does not lie the way the mind can. It keeps a different kind of record.
body:
Fearfully and wonderfully made. I read this verse for years as a fact about biology. It took me a long time to understand it as a fact about memory.

The body kept every room it was in. It kept the weight, the held breath, the years of tension that lived in the shoulders and the clenched jaw at 2am. It also kept the morning the stillness arrived in a coastal town near the water. Your body has been a witness this entire time.

Today: listen to it. It is telling you something you have been talking over.

title: Not resilience. Faithfulness.
quote: Resilience is about bouncing. Faithfulness is about staying.
body:
The verse says do not lose heart. Not do not feel the weight. Not pretend it is not heavy. Just do not lose heart.

There is a difference between resilience and faithfulness that does not get talked about enough. Resilience is a bounce. Faithfulness is a decision not to leave. The man who keeps showing up to a marriage he damaged is not resilient. He is faithful. Those are different muscles and they are built in different ways.

Today: do not try to bounce. Just stay.

title: Dwell with knowledge
quote: To dwell with knowledge means to actually see the person you are sharing the room with.
body:
The instruction is harder than most of what gets preached. Not just live with her. Dwell. According to knowledge. That word means to inhabit the space with full attention to who is in it.

I was in a lot of rooms I was not actually in. The presence was physical. The attention was somewhere behind a screen, or a glass, or a ceiling I was staring at. My wife named it before I could. Not in an argument. In a quiet sentence that did not raise its voice because it did not have to.

Today: look at the person in the room with you. Not at the room. At the person.

title: Cost as evidence
quote: Love is not a feeling. It is what it costs the person who chose it.
body:
Greater love has no one than this. The verse points at the cost, not the feeling. What someone paid is the evidence of what they chose.

I have watched someone pay a cost I am not sure I could have paid in the same position. Not in one large gesture. In the small daily ones. In the patience she did not have to extend. In the morning she showed up anyway.

That is the verse made flesh. Today: notice what someone is paying for you. Name it. Even just to yourself.

title: The number that was never yours
quote: The number on your paycheck taught you how to think about yourself. That was never the number's job.
body:
As a man thinks in his heart, so is he. I read this for years as a verse about positive thinking. Then I understood it as a warning about who I had let do the thinking.

The quiet assessment that ran underneath everything, the arithmetic about who I was and what I was worth, was not from God. It was from a pay stub. From a title. From an institution that had a number for my contribution and that number became my measure.

You are not the number they gave you. Today: question the source of the voice doing the math.

title: New every morning
quote: It is of the Lord's mercies that we are not consumed. Every morning is a new landing.
body:
They are new every morning. Not new every year, or new every major turning point, or new when you finally get it together. Every morning.

The mercy does not accumulate interest. It does not carry a balance from yesterday. It arrives fresh, today, regardless of what yesterday looked like. I have had mornings where the mercy was the only thing that arrived. No plan, no clarity, no momentum. Just the mercy showing up before anything else.

That turned out to be enough. Today: let the morning be the reset, not the reckoning.
